why_v1: Seat anchor keeps action order stable before tournament-pressure decisions.
feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The small blind is the right seat anchor, so the tournament read stays tied to the out-of-position blind who feels the pressure first.
feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. The small blind is the target seat because it is partly committed, out of position, and especially sensitive to ICM pressure. Anchoring a different seat misses where leverage and survival constraints are most distorted.
